signals.reasoning: BTC remains stable with mild trend-supported mechanics, but the active veto keeps it in watch-only territory.
ETH is structurally firm and already moving, which makes it neither a clean fade nor a chase long under the event brake.
SOL still reads more like a short-covering rally than a true trend continuation, so I would not force either side.
PENGU is up enough to be interesting, but not crowded enough to make it a great fade and veto blocks chasing it.
NOT has squeeze-style behavior, but liquidity and spread quality remain too poor to trust under veto.
PUMP has decent liquidity but the move is not clean enough to justify a fresh position while the event brake is active.
ALT has strong squeeze mechanics from negative funding, but liquidity is too thin for a serious fresh bet and veto blocks the chase.
GOAT is one of the cleaner crowded-long fades here because funding is near 1 bp positive and the move looks extended without being fully washed out yet.
DYDX is too flat and too low-quality to justify a fresh trade in this regime.
TON is still one of the best future peak-rejection watches, but it has not actually cracked yet, so under veto it stays watch-only.
VVV is extended and now showing short-covering-rally character, which makes it a reasonable secondary fade candidate if we insist on a short.
